# Carat Capital

> The trade paper of the jewelry world — original, sourced daily reporting on diamonds, gold and precious metals, colored gemstones, watches, jewelry auctions, and jewelry retail and technology. Published at https://caratcapital.org. All articles are original writing with named, linked sources; prices are dated and indicative.

## The paper
- [Front page](https://caratcapital.org/): today's edition, the wire, and the live price tape
- [The Record](https://caratcapital.org/the-record): a dated, sourced week-by-week chronicle of the industry
- [The Almanac](https://caratcapital.org/almanac): the quarter's key numbers in sourced tables (metals, exports, prices, auctions, retail)
- [The Field Guide](https://caratcapital.org/field-guide.html): plain-language introduction to how the jewelry trade works
- [About & editorial standards](https://caratcapital.org/about.html)
- [RSS feed](https://caratcapital.org/feed.xml)
- [Morning Brief newsletter](https://caratcapital.beehiiv.com): free daily email, 06:30 ET

## The six desks
- [Diamonds](https://caratcapital.org/diamonds): the stone that runs on trust
- [Gold & Metals](https://caratcapital.org/gold-metals): the metal beneath every margin
- [Colored Gemstones](https://caratcapital.org/gemstones): supply lines drawn in emerald, ruby and sapphire
- [Watches](https://caratcapital.org/watches): haute horlogerie, priced by the second
- [Auctions & Estates](https://caratcapital.org/auctions): where the trade marks itself to market
- [Retail & Technology](https://caratcapital.org/retail-tech): the counter, rebuilt for the next generation

## Recent articles
- [De Beers digs 88% more, banks 44% less](https://caratcapital.org/a-de-beers-eighty-eight-up): Second-quarter rough output jumped 88% to 7.8 million carats even as the average price fell 32% to $105 a carat. Revenue slid 44% to $665 mi
- [Gold defends $4,000 as oil clears $100](https://caratcapital.org/a-gold-defends-four-thousand-again): Bullion held $4,028 on Friday, a whisker above the $4,000 line, as Brent crude topped $100 and the ten-year yield reached 4.70%. Traders now
- [The Apollo Gallery reopens, minus the crown jewels](https://caratcapital.org/a-apollo-gallery-goes-dark): Nine months after thieves took over $100 million in French royal jewels in a seven-minute raid, the Louvre's Apollo Gallery reopened July 22
- [Emeralds hold at $146 a carat; rubies wait their turn](https://caratcapital.org/a-emeralds-hold-at-146): Gemfields' higher-quality emerald auction took $26.8 million, selling 36 of 37 lots at an average $146.08 a carat. A 10% slide in the rupee 
- [Small stones lead again as one-carat goods slip](https://caratcapital.org/a-small-stones-lead-again): RapNet's June index rose 4.2% for 0.30-carat diamonds and 1.3% for half-carats, while the one-carat benchmark fell 0.7%. Inventory cuts, not
- [Lab-grown now costs 73% less; naturals win the ring back](https://caratcapital.org/a-seventy-three-percent-cheaper): A study of appraisal data finds lab-grown diamonds priced 73% below naturals, up from a 27% gap in 2019. As the discount widened, natural st
- [Trophies carry Sotheby's to $31.4 million; a pink walks](https://caratcapital.org/a-sothebys-thirty-one-million): Sotheby's New York High Jewelry sale made $31.4 million with 95% sold, led by a $3.2 million Graff diamond ring. Down the street, Phillips w
- [Swiss watch exports jump 11.2%, but the profit doesn't follow](https://caratcapital.org/a-exports-up-resale-flat): June's 11.2% export rebound was the strongest reading in a year and sent Swatch shares higher. Yet the group's half-year profit was CHF 16 m
- [Fifty percent for Canada: the wall reaches the diamond counter](https://caratcapital.org/a-fifty-percent-for-canada): A 50% tariff on Canadian goods takes effect August 19 and names six jewelry HTS codes, loose polished diamonds among them. Rough is exempt, 
- [Twelve and a half for color: Washington prices the rainbow](https://caratcapital.org/a-twelve-point-five-for-color): USTR's expedited Section 301 investigation proposes 12.5% tariffs on colored gemstones and cultured pearls from roughly 46 countries, with r
- [Oil talks, gold listens: $4,165 was the top, for now](https://caratcapital.org/a-oil-talks-gold-listens): Gold backed off Wednesday's two-week peak of $4,165.87 to trade $4,103.39 Thursday morning, down 0.6%, as oil hit a six-week high, two-year 
- [Swatch's split screen: nine percent growth, sixteen million profit](https://caratcapital.org/a-swatch-nine-up-six-down): Swatch Group's first half: revenue CHF 3.12 billion, up 2% reported and 9% at constant currency, with the US up 27%, Japan up 20% and Spain 
- [Birks trims the loss by 73 percent: a repair year in Montreal](https://caratcapital.org/a-birks-narrows-by-73): Canada's jeweler of record cut its annual net loss to CAD 3.4 million from CAD 12.8 million, on sales of CAD 205.4 million, up 15.5%, with c
- [The $21,650 lemon: Monica Rich Kosann's fruit that opens](https://caratcapital.org/a-fruit-that-opens): Three pendants in the Enchanted Garden series — a cherry at $10,985, a pear at $14,500 and a lemon at $21,650 — each a hinged locket conceal
- [2,800 square feet for the crown: Tysons gets its Rolex](https://caratcapital.org/a-rolex-on-the-main-corridor): Liljenquist & Beckstead, family jewelers since 1978, opened a 2,800-square-foot Rolex boutique in Tysons Galleria on July 22 — bronze-brass
